tasks.subject: [streaming-message-integrity] [P3] Add getLastMessageIds to AgentRuntime interface and FakeAgentRuntime
tasks.description:
Add the `getLastMessageIds` method to the `AgentRuntime` interface in `packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts` and implement it in `FakeAgentRuntime` in `packages/test-utils`. This is the foundation for server-echo ID reconciliation.

## Files to Modify

### 1. `packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts`

Add the method to the `AgentRuntime` interface, in the "Session queries (storage)" section (after `getSessionETag`):

```typescript
/**
 * Return the JSONL-assigned message IDs for the last user and assistant
 * messages in a session. Used for client-server ID reconciliation.
 *
 * @param sessionId - Session to query
 * @returns ID pair or null if not available
 */
getLastMessageIds(
  sessionId: string,
): Promise<{ user: string; assistant: string } | null>;
```

### 2. `packages/test-utils` — FakeAgentRuntime

Add a `getLastMessageIds` method as a `vi.fn()` stub that returns `null` by default:

```typescript
getLastMessageIds = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(null);
```

This follows the existing pattern in FakeAgentRuntime where every method is a `vi.fn()` spy.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `getLastMessageIds` added to `AgentRuntime` interface with TSDoc
- [ ] `FakeAgentRuntime` implements `getLastMessageIds` as a `vi.fn()` returning `null`
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors across all packages (`pnpm typecheck`)
- [ ] Any class implementing `AgentRuntime` that is missing the method will fail to compile (verified by typecheck)
tasks.activeForm: Adding getLastMessageIds to AgentRuntime interface and FakeAgentRuntime

tasks.subject: [streaming-message-integrity] [P4] Update contributing docs to reflect removed post-stream invalidation pattern
tasks.description:
Update internal contributing documentation that references the post-stream `invalidateQueries` pattern or the seed effect's behavior, since the post-stream reset has been removed.

## Files to Check and Update

### 1. `contributing/data-fetching.md`

Search for references to `invalidateQueries` after streaming, `historySeededRef`, or the post-stream replace pattern. If found, update to describe the new behavior:

- After streaming completes, `historySeededRef.current` stays `true`
- No explicit `queryClient.invalidateQueries` is called
- The seed effect uses Branch 2 (incremental append with tagged-dedup) for all post-stream reconciliation
- The existing `refetchInterval` on the history query handles eventual consistency

### 2. `contributing/architecture.md`

Search for references to the seed effect's three-branch structure or the post-stream history replace. If documented, update to reflect:

- Branch 1: Initial seed (historySeededRef is false, not streaming) — full `setMessages(history.map(...))`
- Branch 2: Incremental append with tagged-dedup (historySeededRef is true, not streaming) — matches tagged messages by content/position, appends new ones
- The post-stream reset that forced Branch 1 on stream completion has been removed
- Tagged-dedup with `_streaming` flag handles client-server ID reconciliation

### 3. Inline code comments

Verify that any comments in `use-chat-session.ts` that reference the old behavior are updated. The removed lines had explanatory comments — ensure no stale references remain.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `contributing/data-fetching.md` updated if it references post-stream invalidation
- [ ] `contributing/architecture.md` updated if it documents the seed effect
- [ ] No stale comments in `use-chat-session.ts` referencing the removed reset pattern
- [ ] Documentation accurately describes the new tagged-dedup behavior
tasks.activeForm: Updating contributing docs to reflect removed post-stream invalidation
